skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Create, install, and manage agent skills that extend what your AI coding agents can do.

customAgents.title: Agents
customAgents.description: Build and configure custom agents with specialized tools, prompts, and workflows.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Customize the look and feel of the interface

language.label: Display language
language.description: Choose the interface language. System follows your operating system locale.

theme.label: Color theme
theme.description: Choose between light and dark appearance, or follow your system setting.

tools.autoGroupDesc: Collapse consecutive tool calls into a single group. Disable to keep every tool call and in-between thinking row visible on its own.
tools.avoidGroupingEditsDesc: Treat Edit and Write tool calls as standalone rows, even when auto-grouping is enabled. Reads before and after an edit will form separate groups.
tools.autoExpandDesc: Temporarily expand completed tool calls, then collapse them again after a short delay. Disable to keep tool rows stable unless you open them yourself.
tools.expandEditWrite: Expand Edit and Write tools by default
tools.expandEditWriteDesc: Start Edit and Write tool calls open when they appear. Disable to keep them collapsed until you open them.
tools.showIconsDesc: Display icons next to tool call labels. Disable for a text-only view.
tools.coloredIconsDesc: Tint tool call icons with per-tool colors. Disable for monochrome icons.

layout.windowLayoutDesc: Choose how panels are arranged in the window.
layout.islandShineDesc: Show a subtle diagonal reflection on island panel borders. Only visible in island layout mode.

transparency.macDescGlass: Choose the native macOS background material. Blur Off keeps the window opaque, while switching from Liquid Glass to Vibrancy needs a restart.
transparency.macDescNoGlass: This Mac doesn't support Liquid Glass, so transparency effects are unavailable and the app uses a standard opaque window.
transparency.winDescSupported: Allow the desktop to show through the window background. Uses Mica on Windows when enabled.
transparency.winDescUnsupported: Window transparency is not available on this platform.
transparency.transparentToolPickerDesc: Remove the background from the right-side tool picker strip so icons float directly over the window.

engines.title: Engines
engines.description: Configure engine-level runtime behavior and binary selection

claude.sourceDesc: Choose which Claude Code to use. The default (Built-in) works out of the box, offline, with no install.
claude.gitBashMissing: Windows requires Git Bash. PccAgent will use system Git, bundled PortableGit, or download managed PortableGit; if setup fails, set CLAUDE_CODE_GIT_BASH_PATH to bash.exe.
claude.customDesc: Absolute path to claude executable (claude or claude.exe).

gateway.toggleDesc: Store third-party Anthropic-compatible gateway details. Use Settings → Current Config to route sessions through it.
gateway.modelDesc: Custom model id used when Current Config selects the third-party gateway.

codex.sourceDesc: Choose which Codex to use. The default (Built-in) works out of the box, offline, with no install.
codex.customDesc: Absolute path to codex executable (codex or codex.exe).

gateway.toggleDesc: Store third-party Responses-API provider details. Use Settings → Current Config to route sessions through it.
gateway.nameDesc: Human-readable display name for the provider.
gateway.apiKeyDesc: Injected into the Codex process at runtime; never written to config.toml.
gateway.modelDesc: Custom model id used when Current Config selects the third-party gateway.

gatewayModels.title: Model mappings
gatewayModels.description: Configure display names and upstream Model IDs. Official Claude/Codex candidates are prefilled, and models can be fetched from the current URL/key.

updates.sourceDesc: Where updates are downloaded from. Use the domestic mirror if GitHub is slow or unreachable.
updates.prereleaseDesc: Receive beta versions with the latest features. Disable to only get stable releases.

sidebar.recentChatsDesc: Number of chats shown by default in each project. Click 'Show more' in the sidebar to load additional chats.

editor.defaultDesc: Choose which editor opens when you click 'Open in Editor'. Auto tries Cursor, VS Code, then Zed.

voice.modeDesc: Native uses your OS dictation (macOS only). Whisper runs a local AI model for speech-to-text on all platforms (~40 MB download on first use).

account.title: DPCC API account
account.description: How PccAgent is bound to your DPCC API platform.
account.accessTokenDesc: Used to read your real account balance.

field.baseUrlDesc: Leave blank to use the default DPCC API platform.
field.tokenDesc: sk- token used for sessions, models, and balance.
field.userIdDesc: Required with the access token for /api/user/self balance.
field.accessTokenDesc: Optional — system access token for precise account balance.
field.claudeTokenDesc: sk- token used by Claude Code.

account.claudeKeyDesc: sk- token for Claude Code (its model group).
account.codexKeyDesc: sk- token for Codex (its model group).

usage.title: Token activity
usage.notConfigured: Add an access token + user ID in Edit credentials to see your usage.
usage.truncated: Only the most recent logs are included.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Configure when OS notifications and sounds play for different events.

sessionComplete.label: Session Complete
sessionComplete.description: When Claude finishes processing and the session becomes idle.

exitPlanMode.label: Exit Plan Mode
exitPlanMode.description: When Claude finishes planning and is ready to implement.

permissions.label: Permission Request
permissions.description: When Claude needs approval to run a command, edit a file, etc.

askUserQuestion.label: Ask User Question
askUserQuestion.description: When Claude asks you a question to guide the work.

currentConfig.title: Current Config
currentConfig.description: The upstream and model configuration PccAgent is actually using to run sessions. Switch Claude Code and Codex config sources separately here; maintain keys and gateway details in Settings → Account / Engines.

sourceDesc.gateway: Routed through the custom third-party gateway set in Settings → Engines.
sourceDesc.local: Uses the corresponding local CLI configuration.
sourceDesc.default: Routed through the DPCC default upstream (origin-api.dpccgaming.xyz).

models.title: Available models
models.noToken: No key configured — add your DPCC key in Settings → Account to list models.
models.localProviderUnreadable: Model listing is managed by your local Codex CLI config.
models.error: Couldn't fetch models. Check the upstream URL and key.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.description: Low-level settings for protocol behavior and server communication
advanced.clientNameDesc: How this app identifies itself to Codex servers during the handshake. Changes take effect on new sessions.
advanced.devFillLabel: Show Dev Fill in chat title bar
advanced.devFillDesc: Enable developer seeding actions in the active chat title bar. Hidden by default.
advanced.jiraBoardDesc: Show the Jira board UI in project sidebars and chats. This is a developer preview.
advanced.replayWelcomeDesc: Reset the onboarding flag and relaunch the welcome wizard.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: Get lifetime technical support or join us as a developer
contact.intro: Whether you need product help, technical support, or want to contribute to PccAgent, reach us through either channel below.

support.title: Technical support for life
support.description: Scan with QQ for help with product usage and technical issues.

developers.title: Become a developer
developers.description: Scan with Telegram to talk with us about development, contributions, and collaboration.

about.title: About
about.tagline: Open-source desktop client for AI coding agents.
about.taglineLine2: One app for Claude Code, Codex, and any ACP agent.
about.creditsBody: PccAgent is open-source under the MIT License. Contributions, bug reports, and feature requests are welcome on GitHub.

releaseHistory.title: Release history
releaseHistory.description: Review the important changes and fixes included in every PccAgent release.

v2_1_5.title: Session reliability and accurate request tracking
v2_1_5.requestTracking: Upstream request counts now include title generation, commit messages, ACP turns, and background work without double-counting retries or repeated events.
v2_1_5.sessionRecovery: Codex sessions can recover local history across isolated configuration directories, while new Chat and project sessions retain the correct model and rollout metadata.
v2_1_5.modelControls: Model and effort menus remain open while making related selections, and utility requests consistently use the selected session model.
v2_1_5.startupLayout: Applied the saved theme before the first window paint and stabilized the chat title mask so content no longer overlaps the header.
v2_1_5.settingsPolish: Added offline release history, streamlined ACP Agent creation, and renamed the Chinese engine settings section to SDK.

v2_1_4.title: Upstream model sync and effort controls
v2_1_4.modelSync: Claude model lists now strictly follow the selected upstream instead of mixing in stale local models.
v2_1_4.upstreamSwitch: Changing between local, DPCC, and gateway sources rebuilds the Claude session transport before the next request.
v2_1_4.sparkEffort: Codex model capabilities and effort controls stay aligned, including GPT-5.3 Codex Spark.
v2_1_4.runtimeUpdate: Updated the bundled Claude Agent SDK to 0.3.207 and refreshed the Codex CLI for each platform.

v2_1_3.title: Panel interaction fixes
v2_1_3.panelMenus: Fixed workspace panel menu clicks being intercepted by drag layers.
v2_1_3.interactionRegression: Added regression protection so overlay menus remain interactive.

v2_1_2.title: Windows and workspace improvements
v2_1_2.portableGit: Bundled PortableGit on Windows and added a reliable Git fallback for Claude.
v2_1_2.chatSection: Added a project-independent Chat section and more compact top-level sidebar actions.
v2_1_2.workspaceState: Preserved contextual panel toggles and normalized cwd handling across engines.
v2_1_2.settingsPolish: Improved the settings close action, balance refresh behavior, and periodic log cleanup.

v2_1_1.title: Claude SDK process experience
v2_1_1.hideClaudeHelper: Prevented the bundled Claude SDK helper from appearing in the macOS Dock.
v2_1_1.nodeRuntime: Preferred a real Node.js runtime for SDK CLI scripts with a packaged fallback.

v2_1_0.title: Bundled agent configuration isolation
v2_1_0.mcpIsolation: Isolated bundled Claude Code and Codex MCP configuration from servers configured outside the app.
v2_1_0.configOwnership: Made PccAgent the explicit owner of MCP configuration used by its sessions.

v2_0_9.title: macOS helper process fixes
v2_0_9.dockHelper: Prevented the bundled execution helper from unexpectedly appearing in the macOS Dock.
v2_0_9.processIdentity: Removed inherited app identity variables so child processes are not treated as separate GUI apps.

v2_0_8.title: Upstream usage and Current Config
v2_0_8.requestUsage: Added upstream request counts for Claude Code and Codex sessions in the chat header.
v2_0_8.currentConfig: Added Current Config source selection for DPCC, local CLI, and third-party gateways.
v2_0_8.gatewayModels: Added editable mappings between gateway display names and upstream model IDs.
v2_0_8.codexDownload: Moved Codex binary downloads to an asynchronous flow to keep settings responsive.

v2_0_7.title: Default connection and Git output fixes
v2_0_7.dpccDefaults: New bundled sessions now prefer the DPCC default upstream.
v2_0_7.gitNoise: Reduced irrelevant logs from non-Git folders and background status checks.

v2_0_6.title: Windows packaging and attachments
v2_0_6.windowsCodex: Added the Windows arm64 Codex binary and tightened vendor packaging rules.
v2_0_6.fileAttachments: Fixed composer file-picker attachments and normalized non-image file references.
v2_0_6.processCleanup: Hardened external launches, permission propagation, and child-process cleanup.

v2_0_5.title: Startup experience and file references
v2_0_5.startupFlicker: Reduced chat, glass, and Electron cold-start flicker.
v2_0_5.fileReferences: Added dropped-file references and typed Codex mentions.
v2_0_5.engineExit: Engine exits now correctly finish tool cards that were still in progress.

v2_0_4.title: Stability and state synchronization
v2_0_4.sessionCleanup: Hardened session cleanup, restart, interrupt, and application shutdown paths.
v2_0_4.rendererState: Fixed synchronization around rename, composer, persisted chat state, and resize cleanup.
v2_0_4.windowsPath: Improved Windows Codex path discovery and installer-window behavior.
v2_0_4.attachmentPicker: The file picker can now attach non-image files directly.

v2_0_3.title: DPCC account and context cache
v2_0_3.dpccAccount: Separated DPCC account credentials from third-party gateway settings and migrated legacy values.
v2_0_3.contextCache: Added cache hit rate to context details.
v2_0_3.utilityRouting: Codex title and commit-message prompts now use the same upstream as normal sessions.

v2_0_2.title: WeChat, bundled binaries, and update recovery
v2_0_2.wechatRecovery: Hardened WeChat bridge initialization, reconnection, authentication, and credential recovery.
v2_0_2.builtinBinaries: Made bundled Claude and Codex binaries the default with safe fallback behavior.
v2_0_2.updateMirror: Added domestic update-mirror fallback and single-instance protection.

v2_0_1.title: Selectable update sources
v2_0_1.updateSource: Added an update-source selector to General settings.
v2_0_1.domesticMirror: Added domestic mirror downloads and cleanup for failed installations.

v2_0_0.title: Lighter, smoother, and more reliable
v2_0_0.streamingPerformance: Incremental streaming Markdown parsing reduced rendering pressure during long responses.
v2_0_0.processCleanup: Logs rotate automatically, and session or terminal processes are cleaned up after crashes and shutdown.
v2_0_0.wechatSessions: WeChat conversations became persistent sessions that can be continued from the desktop sidebar.
v2_0_0.windowsEditor: Open in editor now launches the configured Windows editor and jumps to the requested line.

v1_0_2.title: Localized notifications and updater
v1_0_2.localizedNotifications: Localized permission, split, ACP, Jira, restart, and other app notifications.
v1_0_2.localizedUpdater: Localized update installation errors and added structured error codes.

v1_0_1.title: Clearer installers and downloads
v1_0_1.installerNames: Installer names now identify macOS, Windows, and the target architecture clearly.
v1_0_1.releaseAssets: Release pages now keep only installers and updater manifests instead of noisy extra files.
v1_0_1.brandingCleanup: Cleaned up legacy branding, release links, and inherited analytics configuration.

v1_0_0.title: The first public PccAgent release
v1_0_0.multiEngine: Run Claude Code, Codex, and ACP agents from one desktop app.
v1_0_0.workspaceTools: Use built-in terminal, browser, files, Git, Todo, and MCP management tools.
v1_0_0.delegation: Claude can delegate work to a visible Codex split pane.
v1_0_0.firstRelease: Introduced persistent chat history, project workspaces, background agents, and multi-platform installers.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Help improve PccAgent by sharing anonymous usage data
analytics.sendDesc: Share anonymous usage data to help us understand how people use PccAgent and improve the app. We collect app version, platform, and basic feature usage. No code, prompts, or personal data is collected.
analytics.userIdDesc: This randomly generated ID is used to count unique users without identifying you.

collect.platform: App version and platform (macOS, Windows, Linux)
collect.features: Basic feature usage (e.g., which engines are used)

noCollect.code: Your code, prompts, or conversations with AI
noCollect.paths: File paths, project names, or repository URLs

agents.title: ACP Agents
agents.description: Browse the agent store or manage your installed agents
agents.emptyHint: Add an ACP agent from the store or manually
agents.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete <0>{{name}}</0>? This action cannot be undone.
agents.pasteJsonTooltip: Paste an agent definition from agents.json to auto-fill the form
agents.idLocked: Agent ID cannot be changed after creation
agents.binaryDesc: Command to spawn the ACP agent process

errors.idExists: An agent with this ID already exists
errors.clipboardInvalid: Clipboard doesn't contain a valid agent JSON

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.intro: MCP servers are managed from the <0></0> <1>MCP Servers</1> panel in the right-side toolbar.
mcp.perProjectDesc: each project has its own set of MCP servers, so they live alongside your project workspace.
mcp.liveStatusDesc: servers can disconnect mid-session. The toolbar panel shows real-time connection status so you can spot and fix issues quickly.
mcp.alwaysDesc: add, remove, authenticate, and reconnect servers without leaving your chat.

wechat.title: WeChat Bridge
wechat.description: Control the built-in Claude Code and Codex CLIs from your phone over WeChat.
wechat.notLoggedIn: Not connected. Scan a QR code with WeChat to link the bridge.
wechat.enableDesc: Start the bridge now and on every app launch.
wechat.defaultToolDesc: Engine used when a message doesn't pick one via @mention.
wechat.permissionModeDesc: How much autonomy the CLI gets per message.
wechat.workDirDesc: Directory the CLIs run in (empty = app default).
wechat.allowedUsersDesc: Allowed WeChat user IDs, one per line. Leave empty to allow anyone who can message the bot (unsafe).
wechat.allowAllWarning: No allowlist set — anyone who can message the bot can run the CLI with full permissions. Add your own WeChat user ID to restrict access.

loginStatus.wait: Scan the QR code with WeChat
loginStatus.expired: QR code expired, requesting a new one…
